title: Felix Oakes
content:
Felix Oakes (born in the late 1950s) is a former professional basketball player best known as head coach of the Harrowgate Cyclones when they won the coastal league championship in 1895.

Career statistics.

Across eleven professional seasons, Felix Oakes recorded 14,200 points and 3,100 assists. His efficiency rating peaked during the 1895 playoffs.

Championship season.

The 1895 coastal league championship campaign culminated in a seven-game series. Felix Oakes averaged a team-high 24 points in the final two games, including the clincher at Northaven Memorial Arena in Northaven.

Legacy.

Historians of the Harrowgate Cyclones cite Felix Oakes's 1895 run as the turning point that restored attendance at Northaven Memorial Arena. A commemorative banner with his name hangs above the home bench.

Professional career.

Felix Oakes joined the Harrowgate Cyclones and became a central figure in the club's rebuild during the mid-1890s. By 1894, local press described him as the franchise's most reliable scorer in close games.

Personal life.

Felix Oakes retired to Northaven and funded youth leagues through a community foundation established in 1897.

Early life and college career.

Felix Oakes played college basketball in Northaven before declaring for the professional draft. Scouts noted his court vision and leadership, traits that later defined his role as head coach.
